overview.description: Vertex AI Agent Builder is a suite of products that help developers build, scale, and govern AI agents in production. Vertex AI Agent Builder provides a full-stack, secure foundation that supports the entire agent lifecycle.
keyCapabilities: Build agents using frameworks such as Agent Development Kit (ADK) or any other open-source framework
Scale agents into production with built-in testing, release management, and reliability
Govern agents with monitoring, audit trails, and security features

build.description: Build agents using various frameworks and tools

agentDevelopmentKit.name: Agent Development Kit (ADK)
agentDevelopmentKit.description: An open-source framework that simplifies the process of building sophisticated multi-agent systems while maintaining precise control over agent behavior

agentGarden.name: Agent Garden
agentGarden.description: A library in the Google Cloud console where you can find and explore sample agents and tools designed to accelerate development

agents.description: Prebuilt, end-to-end solutions for specific use cases (e.g., customer service, data analysis, creative writing)
agents.usage: Select and deploy to your project

tools.description: Individual components that can be added to your own agents
tools.integration: Can be integrated into agents built with Agent Development Kit (ADK)

scale.description: Scale agents into production with built-in testing, release management, and reliability

vertexAIAgentEngine.name: Vertex AI Agent Engine
vertexAIAgentEngine.description: A set of services that enables developers to deploy, manage, and scale AI agents in production. Agent Engine handles the infrastructure to scale agents in production so you can focus on creating applications.
vertexAIAgentEngine.note: The name of the resource in the API reference is ReasoningEngine to maintain backwards compatibility

runtime.name: Runtime
runtime.description: Deploy and scale agents with a managed runtime and end-to-end management capabilities
features: Customize the agent's container image with build-time installation scripts for system dependencies
Use security features including VPC-SC compliance and configuration of authentication and IAM
Access models and tools such as function calling
Deploy agents built using different Python frameworks and the Agent2Agent open protocol

qualityAndEvaluation.name: Quality and evaluation
qualityAndEvaluation.description: Evaluate agent quality with the integrated Gen AI Evaluation service and optimize agents with Gemini model training runs

exampleStore.name: Example Store
exampleStore.description: Store and dynamically retrieve few-shot examples to improve agent performance

create.title: Create or reuse an Example Store instance

sessions.name: Sessions
sessions.description: Agent Engine Sessions lets you store individual interactions between users and agents, providing definitive sources for conversation context

manageAdk.title: Manage sessions using Agent Development Kit

manageApi.title: Manage sessions using console or API calls

memoryBank.name: Memory Bank
memoryBank.description: Agent Engine Memory Bank lets you store and retrieve information from sessions to personalize agent interactions

codeExecution.name: Code Execution
codeExecution.description: Agent Engine Code Execution lets your agent run code in a secure, isolated, and managed sandbox environment

observability.name: Observability
observability.description: Understand agent behavior with Google Cloud Trace (supporting OpenTelemetry), Cloud Monitoring, and Cloud Logging

governance.name: Governance
governance.description: Vertex AI Agent Engine supports several features to help you govern agents in production and meet your security and enterprise needs

features.name: Agent Engine Threat Detection
features.description: A built-in service of Security Command Center that helps you detect and investigate potential attacks on agents deployed to Vertex AI Agent Engine Runtime

features.name: Agent identity
features.description: Use Identity Access Management (IAM) agent identity to provide security and access management features

steps.description: Set up your Google project and install the latest version of the Vertex AI SDK for Python

steps.description: Develop an agent that can be deployed on Vertex AI Agent Engine

steps.description: Deploy the agent on the Vertex AI Agent Engine managed runtime

steps.description: Manage and delete agents that you have deployed to Vertex AI Agent Engine

customTemplate.description: You can adapt a custom template to support deployment to Vertex AI Agent Engine from your framework

vertexAiSdkIntegration.description: Vertex AI Agent Engine provides managed templates per framework in the Vertex AI SDK and documentation

fullIntegration.description: Features are integrated to work across the framework, Vertex AI Agent Engine, and broader Google Cloud ecosystem

agentStarterPack.name: Agent Starter Pack
agentStarterPack.description: A collection of production-ready generative AI agent templates built for Vertex AI Agent Engine
features: Pre-built agent templates: ReAct, RAG, multi-agent, and other templates
Interactive playground: Test and interact with your agent
Automated infrastructure: Uses Terraform for streamlined resource management
CI/CD pipelines: Automated deployment workflows leveraging Cloud Build
Observability: Built-in support for Cloud Trace and Cloud Logging

useCases.category: Build agents by connecting to public APIs

examples.title: Convert between currencies
examples.description: Create a function that connects to a currency exchange app, allowing the model to provide accurate answers to queries such as "What's the exchange rate for euros to dollars today?"

examples.title: Designing a community solar project
examples.description: Identify potential locations, look up relevant government offices and suppliers, and review satellite images and solar potential of regions and buildings to find the optimal location to install your solar panels

useCases.category: Build agents by connecting to databases

examples.title: Integration with AlloyDB and Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
Build agents with tools that access data in your database
Query and understand structured datastores using natural language
Query and understand graph databases using natural language
Query and understand vector stores using natural language

useCases.category: Build agents with Agent Development Kit

examples.title: Build and deploy agents using Agent Development Kit
Convert between currencies with Express Mode
examples.description: Deploy to Agent Engine without billing with Vertex AI Express mode

examples.title: Manage context with Sessions and Memory Bank in Express Mode
Build and deploy agents using the OneTwo open-source framework
Build and deploy agents using the LangGraph open-source framework
Build and trace agents using OpenTelemetry and Cloud Trace
Build multi-agent systems with A2A protocol (preview)
examples.description: Build interoperable agents that communicate and collaborate with other agents regardless of their framework

enterpriseSecurity.description: Vertex AI Agent Engine supports several features to help you meet enterprise security requirements, adhere to your organization's security policies, and follow security best practices

features.name: VPC Service Controls
features.description: Strengthen data security and mitigate the risks of data exfiltration. When VPC Service Controls is configured, the deployed agent retains secure access to Google APIs and services, such as BigQuery API, Cloud SQL Admin API, and Vertex AI API, verifying seamless operation within your defined perimeter. Critically, VPC Service Controls effectively blocks all public internet access, confining data movement to your authorized network boundaries
features.note: If you use Vertex AI Agent Engine in a VPC-SC environment, you must create an ingress rule in your perimeter to allow ingress from the Reasoning Engine Service Agent (service-PROJECT_NUMBER@gcp-sa-aiplatform-re.iam.gserviceaccount.com) to storage.googleapis.com service and artifactregistry.googleapis.com service

features.name: Private Service Connect interface
features.description: For Vertex AI Agent Engine Runtime, PSC-I lets your agents interact with privately hosted services in a user's VPC

features.name: Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK)
features.description: Protect your data with your own encryption keys, which gives you ownership and full control of the keys that protect your data at rest in Google Cloud

features.name: Data residency (DRZ)
features.description: Ensure that all data at rest and in use are stored within the specified region

features.name: HIPAA
features.description: As a part of Vertex AI Platform, Vertex AI Agent Engine supports HIPAA workloads

features.name: Access Transparency
features.description: Provides you with logs that capture the actions Google personnel take when accessing your content

memoryBank.drzInUse: Yes* (only when using a Gemini regional endpoint)

expressMode.description: Vertex AI Agent Engine supports Vertex AI in express mode. This lets you use Vertex AI Agent Engine without creating a Google Cloud project

features.description: During client side development of your agent, Vertex AI express mode lets you access models from Vertex AI Studio using an API key
In express mode, you can create a ReasoningEngine instance, which lets you use the Session and Memory Bank service
Deploy your agent directly from local source code without using a Cloud Storage bucket
Requires enabling billing on your express mode account because deployment from an agent object requires a Cloud Storage bucket to store agent code and artifacts

expressMode.note: Vertex AI in express mode has different quotas than regular Google Cloud projects. See Express mode quota and system limits for Vertex AI Agent Engine express mode quota information

supportedRegions.description: See Locations for a list of supported regions for Vertex AI Agent Engine

quota.description: See Quota and system limits for Vertex AI Agent Engine quota information

pricing.description: A free tier is available for Vertex AI Agent Engine Runtime

sdkMigration.description: The agent_engines module within the Vertex AI SDK for Python is being refactored to a client-based design
reasons: To align with Google ADK and Google Gen AI SDK in canonical type representations
For client-level scoping of Google Cloud parameters in multi-project multi-location applications
To improve discoverability and cohesiveness of Vertex AI Agent Engine services
sdkMigration.note: See the Agent Engine SDK migration guide for instructions to migrate to the client-based Vertex AI SDK for Python if you are still on a version of google-cloud-aiplatform before 1.112.0

keyConcepts.aiAgents: AI agents are autonomous systems that can perform tasks, make decisions, and interact with their environment
keyConcepts.multiAgentSystems: Systems where multiple agents work together to accomplish complex tasks
keyConcepts.agentLifecycle: The complete process from building to deploying to governing agents
keyConcepts.rag: Retrieval-Augmented Generation - combining retrieval of information with generative AI
keyConcepts.mcp: Model Context Protocol - a protocol for tools and context management
keyConcepts.observability: The ability to understand agent behavior through tracing, monitoring, and logging
